content: Curiosity-driven intrinsic reward mechanisms (from Topic A's ICM-based autonomous agents) can be repurposed to prioritize which new unlabeled data samples get stored into an active learning agent's long-term memory, creating a self-sustaining memory pruning loop that outperforms static SOTA 2024 retention heuristics. Instead of storing all newly labeled training data, the agent only retains samples that trigger intrinsic curiosity, aligning memory growth with active learning's core goal of maximizing model improvement per unit of storage.
